Title: The role of VvMYBA2r and VvMYBA2w alleles of the MYBA2 locus in the regulation of anthocyanin biosynthesis for molecular breeding of grape (Vitis spp.) skin coloration

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Stress-associated protein; putative E3 ubiquitin ligase-related factor
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Skin coloration and stress protection discussed for anthocyanin
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: No direct anthocyanin phenotype established; specifically interacts with nonfunctional VvMYBA2w and is proposed to relate to stress/ubiquitination pathways
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvSAP5 was identified as a specific interactor of VvMYBA2w but not VvMYBA2r. 2. Interaction was validated by Y2H and BiFC in nuclei. 3. Authors propose involvement in ubiquitination-mediated stress responses rather than direct anthocyanin activation.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Function in anthocyanin regulation remains speculative
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Currently limited; may be relevant for studying stress-related roles of the defective VvMYBA2w allele
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as possible component of abiotic stress tolerance/protein degradation pathway

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Skin coloration and stress protection discussed for anthocyanin
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: No direct anthocyanin phenotype established; specifically interacts with nonfunctional VvMYBA2w and may connect it to ubiquitination-related processes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvUBE2A specifically interacts with VvMYBA2w but not VvMYBA2r. 2. The interaction was confirmed in yeast and plant cells. 3. Authors suggest a possible role in VvMYBA2w-linked ubiquitination/protein turnover pathways.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Function in metabolite control is speculative and not directly validated
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Currently limited to mechanistic studies of VvMYBA2w-associated pathways
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not shown to directly regulate anthocyanin accumulation in this study

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Berry skin/pericarp; tobacco corolla, stamen, leaf
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to grape skin coloration; associated with plant stress protection as discussed for anthocyanins
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation via MBW complex formation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: VvUFGT; VvLDOX; VvF3H1; VvOMT; in transgenic tobacco NtUFGT, NtCHI, NtCHS, NtDFR, NtF3H, NtF3'0H, NtOMT, NtANS
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates anthocyanin biosynthetic structural genes, especially strongly induces UFGT
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Berry developmental/ripening signals; expression increases after veraison
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low at 15–30 DAA, then continuously increases until maturity; highly co-expressed with VvUFGT in Heimeiren berries
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In grape berry skin during ripening; transgene expressed in tobacco leaf, corolla and stamen
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Functional MYBA2 allele and positive regulator decisive for anthocyanin accumulation and dark skin color
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation and darker pigmentation; overexpression increases anthocyanin in tobacco, especially flowers; functional allele associated with dark grape skin
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in transgenic tobacco; qRT-PCR of pathway genes; dual-luciferase assay on VvUFGT promoter; yeast transactivation assay; Y2H; BiFC; transient co-expression in tobacco leaves; haplotype-color association in 213 germplasms
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Berry developmental stage/ripening; transient agroinfiltration assays in tobacco leaves
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter; VvUFGT promoter in reporter assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvMYBA2r is a functional R2R3-MYB activator localized in the nucleus. 2. Overexpression in tobacco strongly increases anthocyanin accumulation, especially in corollas and stamens. 3. It up-regulates multiple anthocyanin structural genes and directly activates the VvUFGT promoter. 4. It interacts with VvMYCA1 and, together with VvWDR1, forms an MBW complex that enhances anthocyanin biosynthesis. 5. Its expression rises during berry ripening and correlates positively with anthocyanin-pathway genes. 6. Functional MYBA2 haplotypes contribute to darker grape skin colour and can be used in breeding prediction.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression in tobacco caused delayed anther dehiscence, altered endothecial lignification, and chlorosis in transiently infiltrated leaves
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker/target for early prediction of grape skin color and for molecular breeding or engineering of darker, anthocyanin-rich grape varieties; stacking/selection with functional MYBA1 alleles and haplotypes may optimize berry color
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: VvMYBA2r alone activated VvUFGT promoter ~5-fold and with VvMYCA1 ~60-fold; stronger activation with Arabidopsis EGL3 than VvMYCA1 in assay context

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB TF with truncated C-terminus; carries R44L substitution and CA dinucleotide deletion
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Berry skin/pericarp; tested in tobacco corolla, stamen, leaf
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to grape skin coloration when pathway is active; anthocyanins also discussed as stress-protective metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Loss of transcriptional activation and loss of bHLH interaction; defective regulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: VvUFGT (fails to activate effectively); anthocyanin pathway genes broadly not induced
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Fails to significantly induce anthocyanin biosynthetic genes or activate VvUFGT promoter
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Does not form functional complex with VvMYCA1; specifically interacts with VvSAP5 and VvUBE2A
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Berry developmental context; transcript level lower than VvMYBA2r in Shine Muscat during berry development
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed at low levels throughout developing berries of white Shine Muscat, lower than VvMYBA2r
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed in tobacco leaf, corolla and stamen but without obvious pigmentation effect
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Nonfunctional MYBA2 allele contributing to loss of anthocyanin accumulation and white skin color
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Does not significantly promote anthocyanin accumulation; associated with white-skinned grapes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in transgenic tobacco; qRT-PCR of pathway genes; yeast transactivation assay; Y2H; BiFC; dual-luciferase assay; mutational dissection with VvMYBA2r-derived mutants
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Berry developmental stage/ripening; transient agroinfiltration assays in tobacco leaves
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter; VvUFGT promoter in reporter assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvMYBA2w is a nucleus-localized but defective MYB allele from white grape. 2. Ectopic expression in tobacco does not significantly increase anthocyanin accumulation or induce pathway genes. 3. It lacks transcriptional activation because CA dinucleotide deletion truncates the C-terminal activation region. 4. R44L substitution abolishes interaction with the bHLH partner VvMYCA1. 5. The two mutations act additively to eliminate activation of VvUFGT and anthocyanin biosynthesis. 6. VvMYBA2w specifically interacts with VvSAP5 and VvUBE2A, suggesting an alternative stress/protein-turnover-related role.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Transient expression caused chlorosis in tobacco leaves when co-infiltrated in some combinations; no useful pigmentation enhancement
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Serves as a diagnostic nonfunctional allele for predicting white/light berry skin color in breeding; mutation sites are useful molecular markers
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: R44L and CA deletion are additive in impairing function; although nuclear-localized, VvMYBA2w cannot interact with VvMYCA1 or activate VvUFGT effectively

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Berry skin/pericarp; tobacco leaf transient assay
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Grape skin coloration; anthocyanin-associated stress protection discussed generally
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: VvUFGT promoter via cooperation with VvMYBA2r
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Continuously increases during berry development, peaks at 75 DAA, then declines at 95 DAA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Studied in grape berries and transiently in tobacco leaves
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential bHLH partner enabling strong activation of anthocyanin biosynthesis by VvMYBA2r
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances anthocyanin accumulation when combined with VvMYBA2r, and more strongly with VvWDR1 added
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; dual-luciferase assay; transient co-expression in tobacco leaves; expression analysis during berry development
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Berry development; transient agroinfiltration in tobacco leaves
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S in expression constructs; VvUFGT promoter reporter
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvMYCA1 physically interacts with VvMYBA2r and VvWDR1. 2. It is required as a bHLH partner for strong activation of the VvUFGT promoter by VvMYBA2r. 3. Co-expression with VvMYBA2r enhances anthocyanin-related responses in tobacco assays. 4. Interaction is abolished with defective VvMYBA2w carrying the R44L substitution.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential co-engineering component with VvMYBA2r to maximize anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: VvMYCA1 alone had limited activation; synergistic effect with VvMYBA2r was strong (~60-fold promoter activation)

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Berry skin/pericarp; tobacco leaf transient assay
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Grape skin coloration; anthocyanin-associated stress protection discussed generally
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin pathway via enhancement of VvMYBA2r-VvMYCA1 complex activity
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enhances activation of anthocyanin biosynthesis indirectly through MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mildly fluctuates during berry development, decreasing at 30 and 95 DAA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Studied in grape berries and transiently in tobacco leaves
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Enhancer of anthocyanin accumulation through stabilization/assembly of functional MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances anthocyanin accumulation beyond the VvMYBA2r-VvMYCA1 combination
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; transient co-expression in tobacco leaves; expression analysis during berry development
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Berry development; transient agroinfiltration in tobacco leaves
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvWDR1 physically interacts with VvMYCA1 in vitro and in vivo. 2. It participates in the grape MBW complex with VvMYBA2r and VvMYCA1. 3. Co-expression of VvWDR1 with VvMYBA2r and VvMYCA1 causes earlier and stronger anthocyanin accumulation in tobacco leaves than the binary combination. 4. It acts as an enhancer of anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be considered in gene stacking to further boost anthocyanin production together with MYB and bHLH partners
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts mainly as complex enhancer rather than primary determinant
